discharge_summary: Patient is a 58-year-old male with type 2 diabetes mellitus admitted for diabetic ketoacidosis. Social history reveals recent job loss and inability to afford insulin medication. Patient lives in a studio apartment with his elderly mother and two adult children due to housing instability. He has been rationing his insulin due to financial constraints. Discharge planning includes referral to endocrinology, diabetes education, and social work for medication assistance programs and housing resources.
admission_notes: Housing instability, medication non-compliance due to financial barriers

discharge_summary: 45-year-old female with hypertension and chronic kidney disease presents with uncontrolled blood pressure. Patient completed high school but reports difficulty understanding complex medication regimens. Lives in a rural area 45 minutes from nearest healthcare facility. Has been missing follow-up appointments due to lack of reliable transportation and inability to take time off work without pay. Works two part-time jobs without health insurance benefits.
admission_notes: Transportation barriers, low health literacy, employment challenges

discharge_summary: 72-year-old male with COPD and heart failure. Patient lives alone in a senior housing complex but reports feeling socially isolated since his wife passed away six months ago. Has no close family nearby and limited social support network. Neighbors check on him occasionally. Patient admits to poor medication adherence and missed appointments due to depression and lack of motivation. Needs referral to mental health services and community support programs.
admission_notes: Social isolation, bereavement, mental health needs

discharge_summary: 29-year-old mother with postpartum depression and anxiety. Lives in a neighborhood with high crime rates and limited access to mental health services. Concerned about leaving her infant with childcare to attend therapy appointments. Partner works long hours to support the family financially. Patient reports stress about making ends meet and affording healthy food for the family. Breastfeeding mother avoiding medications due to concerns about infant safety.
admission_notes: Neighborhood safety concerns, childcare barriers, food insecurity

discharge_summary: 63-year-old construction worker with chronic back pain and history of substance abuse. Recently injured on job site but employer disputes workers compensation claim. Patient has been self-medicating with alcohol due to pain and stress. Lives in a mobile home park with poor air quality due to nearby industrial facilities. Limited education (8th grade) and difficulty navigating healthcare system and legal processes for workers compensation.
admission_notes: Occupational health hazards, environmental exposure, limited health literacy, legal barriers
